default_summaries.grad: Recent First-Class MSc Computer Science (NLP) graduate from DCU (Dean's Honour List, top 5%) with a portfolio of hands-on AI projects — a neuro-symbolic legal-reasoning pipeline, transformer fine-tuning for financial NER, and an autonomous job-search copilot. Strong analytical foundation from 13 years of prior professional experience; looking for graduate-level engineering roles where I can ship and learn fast.
default_summaries.tech_eng: NLP/ML Engineer building practical AI systems — an autonomous job-search copilot, a neuro-symbolic legal-reasoning pipeline with verifiable symbolic logic, and transformer fine-tuning for financial NER. First-Class MSc CS (NLP) @DCU, top 5%; 13 years of prior experience architecting high-concurrency, national-scale tax data infrastructure.
default_summaries.regtech: RegTech / Compliance AI Engineer combining 13 years of tax-administration and regulatory experience (Shanghai Municipal Taxation Bureau — Golden Tax Project Phases III & IV, national 12366 hotline systems) with NLP and LLM engineering capability. First-Class MSc CS (NLP) from DCU (top 5%); built a neuro-symbolic legal-reasoning pipeline with Walkers Global and a domain-NER fine-tuning project on financial entities.

details: Coursework: Machine Learning, Deep Learning, LLMs, NLP, Data Science
Industry-linked projects with Quantexa, Trivago, aiXplain, and Walkers Global

education.degree: B.Sc. Information Management & Information Systems | LL.B. in Law (Dual Degree)

experience.title: Research Intern
bullets: Evaluated open-source autonomous networking AI frameworks, focusing on trustworthiness and explainability gaps that hinder large-scale deployment in the telecoms sector.
Conducted competitive analysis of agentic AI offerings from leading vendors (Datadog, Cisco and peers) to benchmark capabilities and inform Huawei's L4 autonomous-networking research direction.
Authored technical insight reports on SOTA AI techniques, translating complex architectures into risk assessments and strategic implications for both technical and business stakeholders.
Researched agentic AI and autonomous networking (L4) approaches, contributing to next-generation intelligent operations and maintenance systems.

experience.title: Masters Practicum: Neuro-Symbolic Legal Reasoning
bullets: Architected a multi-stage Neuro-Symbolic pipeline to automate conversion of US Tax Code (SARA) into executable Prolog predicates, achieving 64% end-to-end accuracy.
Developed a deterministic reasoning engine using Python, SWI-Prolog and LLMs (GPT-4o, Gemini 2.5 Pro) that replaces redundant LLM calls with a verified symbolic knowledge base.
Engineered an automated feedback loop with syntactic/semantic checks and execution-based evaluation, boosting syntactic correctness to over 90%.
Collaborated with Walkers Global on LLM-powered legal service automation research.

experience.title: Senior Specialist / Tax Officer
bullets: Self-taught Python (from 2018) and SQL while in role; applied both to build internal RPA tooling on JavaScript-driven government platforms, automating single-point data entry and field population across systems; later completed FreeCodeCamp's frontend curriculum (2022–2023).

Designed a SQL full-text retrieval system and built a structured Q&A knowledge base for the national 12366 tax-counseling hotline, organising operator queries with verified answers; recognised as top 1% performer.

Served on the national task force for China's Golden Tax Project (Phases III & IV), helping architect technical and data frameworks for the national tax-infrastructure rollout with high-concurrency reliability and scalability requirements.
Conducted risk-based financial audits on corporate transaction data, identifying fraud indicators and compliance anomalies; generated municipal revenue forecasts and anomaly flags.

projects.title: GliNER Fine-tuning for Financial NER
bullets: Fine-tuned GliNER LLMs for domain-specific NER in zero-shot and few-shot financial scenarios.
Built a data curation pipeline using FAISS + Sentence Transformers to select 1,000 high-quality financial records from 100k raw samples for training.
Built an end-to-end data labeling pipeline using Argilla for gold-standard evaluation.

projects.title: Neuro-Symbolic Pipeline for Statutory Reasoning
bullets: Developed a framework to convert the US Tax Code into Prolog logic predicates using LLMs and Chain-of-Thought (CoT), achieving verifiable statutory reasoning.
Implemented a symbolic verification layer to reduce hallucinations and improve logical consistency.
Built automated validation with syntactic/semantic checks and self-verification mechanisms.

projects.title: AI-Powered Movie Recommendation Agent
bullets: Built an autonomous agent on the aiXplain platform that recommends movies by orchestrating Movie DB, streaming availability, and translation APIs using a ReAct loop.
Implemented session-aware context management to maintain coherent multi-turn conversations across API calls.

projects.title: JobPilot – AI Job Search Copilot
projects.tech: Python, LangGraph, Claude API, FastAPI, LaTeX
bullets: Built a personal AI copilot that searches jobs across LinkedIn and 9 Greenhouse/Lever/Ashby ATS sources, maintains a career story bank, and generates tailored CVs and cover letters as PDFs.

Implemented LLM-powered story matching with keyword fallback, FastAPI backend exposing pipeline data via REST endpoints, LangGraph 15-node orchestration (evolved from an earlier Greenhouse/Lever ATS-scanner v0), and an ATS keyword-gap scoring loop that drives CV re-tailoring.
Maintained 120+ unit and integration tests with GitHub Actions CI covering LaTeX rendering, story-bank CRUD, dedup filtering, and ATS discovery sources.

projects.title: Personal AI Assistant (Telegram, Raspberry Pi)
projects.tech: Python, Anthropic SDK / Claude Code CLI, Telegram API, Docker
bullets: Built and deployed a 24/7 bilingual (English/Chinese) personal AI assistant on Raspberry Pi via Telegram — captures, queries, and updates todos in natural language with context-aware multi-turn conversation; daily morning digest push.
Engineered a pluggable LLMBackend abstraction (Claude Code CLI / Anthropic SDK) and plaintext-markdown storage (no database, version-controllable, hand-editable); ~970 LoC Python in Docker on Pi.
Maintained engineering discipline with 4 architecture decision records (ADRs), CHANGELOG, and FIELDNOTES tracking design decisions across multi-month daily-driven iteration.

projects.title: Crypto Price & Volatility Modeling
bullets: Built time-series features and evaluated predictive models for price direction/volatility with time-aware validation.
Used Tableau to visualise datasets and modelling results for feature selection and segment analysis.

projects.title: Topic Modeling on CORD-19
projects.tech: Python, LDA, BERTopic, PyTorch, LLMs, Bayesian Optimization
bullets: Compared LDA vs BERTopic on the CORD-19 corpus, weighing coherence/efficiency tradeoffs; tuned α, β, and topic count via Bayesian Optimization.
Built an LLM-based topic-labelling pipeline using GPT-4o, Claude 3.5, and Llama 3.2; evaluated qualitative coherence with DeepSeek-R1 + cosine similarity.
Tracked compute footprint across model variants with CodeCarbon CO2 benchmarking.
Coordinated team work on dataset processing and experiment tracking.

projects.title: Visa Appointment Checker Bot
bullets: Built a monitoring bot that checks Schengen visa appointment availability on BLS Spain Global, sending real-time alerts via email and Telegram.
Implemented configurable scheduling, error handling, and deployable via systemd/cron for continuous monitoring.

projects.title: Festival Greetings Generator (WeChat Mini Program)
projects.tech: WeChat WXML/WXSS, JavaScript, Python FastAPI, Deepseek/Llama-3
bullets: Built a full-stack WeChat Mini Program with Python FastAPI backend that generates personalized festival greetings using open-source LLMs (Deepseek, Llama-3).
Integrated LLM API for natural language generation with a mobile-first frontend serving real users.

projects.title: Multi-Model Question Answering on TweetQA
projects.tech: Python, PyTorch, BERT, LSTM, GPT, spaCy
bullets: Implemented and compared 4 QA approaches on 10,692 tweet samples: rule-based baseline, LSTM, fine-tuned BERT, and GPT prompting; addressed an OOV problem affecting 23% of unique tokens.
Evaluated with BLEU-1, METEOR, and ROUGE-L metrics; fine-tuned BERT reached ROUGE-L 0.494, demonstrating progressive improvement from heuristics to transformer-based models.

awards: 2nd Place, Coding Civilization Intervarsity Programming Competition, Maynooth University, 2025
Dean's Honour List (Top 5%), Dublin City University, 2025
Top 1% Performer, National 12366 Tax Counseling Hotline, Shanghai Municipal Taxation Bureau
